title: So if you were an Evangelion DVD where would you hide
display_title: So if you were an Evangelion DVD where would you hide
wikitext:
[]: So, if you were an Evangelion DVD, where would you hide?             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Jan 08, 2007 3:52 pm God damn it... After all this Eva discussion and fanwanking, I decided to watch the series again. Of course, after I finish the first DVD, and I pop open the second case to find that it's gone. Poof. Checked all the DVD players, nowhere to be found. I figure it's in my black leather CD case, but I can't find that either. This is an urgency! And i'm running out of ideas...<_<

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 08, 2007 4:02 pm Pick a place and start cleaning. Continue until the DVD shows up. So, if you were an Evangelion DVD, where would you hide?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Jan 08, 2007 4:43 pm Check in your other Eva DVD cases or any other Anime series cases that you may have been watching last time you watched the series. If I loose a dvd that is usually where mine seem to be hiding.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Jan 08, 2007 4:54 pm Well, I checked all of my CD cases, all my DVD cases, found the black leather case and it wasn't there, nowhere. My life is empty, I no longer have a complete set of Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Jan 08, 2007 4:58 pm Hah! Found it! Wow, i'm stupid. I left it in my computer. I don't hear disc read noises, so I never thought to check there. Thank God, my life has a purpose again! BTW, lock this, i'm done. *breathes sigh of relief*

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Jan 08, 2007 4:59 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Wow, i'm stupid. I left it in my computer.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 08, 2007 5:31 pm No matter how hard I look, I can't find my platinum vol. 5 dvd. It's been missing for quite a few months now.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Jan 08, 2007 6:01 pm Shitty deal. How hard have ya looked? I've lost things in the weirdest places... Shame, too. That DVD has some of the better episodes in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Mon Jan 08, 2007 7:09 pm All of my Eva DVDs are sitting on this desk, about 3 feet away from me. Unfortunately, the EoE one is the glitched one that I STILL haven't gotten around to replacing yet...

[]:             Quote Post by zephyr72 » Tue Jan 09, 2007 12:32 am The Eva Monkey wrote: No matter how hard I look, I can't find my platinum vol. 5 dvd. It's been missing for quite a few months now.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 09, 2007 12:40 am zephyr72 wrote: Did you lend it to a friend? I've lost a few discs that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Tue Jan 09, 2007 12:54 am The Eva Monkey wrote: zephyr72 wrote: Did you lend it to a friend? I've lost a few discs that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Jan 09, 2007 1:09 am I would hide in another dimension, which is where I think some of my things dissapears to every now and then since I'm very through in keeping track of places where I put stuff, yet my things dissapear inexplicably every now and then. Yep, definatly stolen by phantom dvd theives and taken to another dimension!

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Jan 09, 2007 4:46 pm I tend to lose some of some of my DVD's under my bed. Then when I go looking for them I find my missing socks too.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:05 pm Am i the only one that never loses the dvds? I keep them safe in my library! And make sure that no one touches them!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:08 pm I never lose DVDs. Whenever I get a new one I rip all the audio tracks and the video onto our video server and store the DVD away as a backup.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:10 pm Something like that would require responsibly putting things away after we are done with them. From a male point of view I think I would be in violation of a man law doing something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:13 pm Hunter21 wrote: Something like that would require responsibly putting things away after we are done with them. From a male point of view I think I would be in violation of a man law doing something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Nope. I have a fairly strict no-lending policy. Each of the last 3 things I lent took in excess of a year to get back.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Jan 09, 2007 5:58 pm I haven't lost a disc yet, though I do have an inexplicable surplus of Evangelion Platinum Vol. 5 DVDs.
